meta.title: GCSE Revision Planner | Study Hour
meta.description: Study Hour is a GCSE revision planner that helps students decide what to revise next based on exam timing, weaker topics, and recent study.
meta.ogDescription: A GCSE revision planner that helps students know what to study next instead of relying on a static timetable.
meta.twitterDescription: Plan GCSE revision around exam timing, weaker topics, and recent study.

hero.headline: GCSE revision planner that helps you know what to study next
hero.intro: Study Hour helps GCSE students build a revision plan around exam timing, weaker topics, and recent study, so revision feels directed instead of random.

problem.title: Why GCSE revision planning is hard
points: Static revision timetables become outdated quickly as exams get closer and priorities change.
Students often spend too much time on familiar subjects because they feel easier to start.
It is hard to know what matters most today without checking exam dates, weak topics, and recent study together.

contrast.title: Why static timetables fall short
points: A timetable can tell you when to revise, but not whether you are revising the right topic.
Template-based schedules rarely adapt to weaker or neglected areas.
When the plan stops feeling relevant, students ignore it instead of trusting it.

points.title: Exam timing stays visible
points.description: Upcoming papers still matter, but they are balanced against what you have actually been studying.

points.title: Weak topics rise to the top
points.description: Low-confidence or lower-performing topics surface before they become last-minute panic revision.

points.title: Recent study still counts
points.description: Topics you have ignored recently can regain priority, so revision stays balanced over time.

faq.question: What makes this different from a revision timetable?
faq.answer: A revision timetable gives structure, but Study Hour also helps decide what to revise next based on exam timing, weaker topics, and recent study.

faq.question: Do I need an account?
faq.answer: No. Study Hour works without login, ads, or tracking.

faq.question: Is it only for GCSE students?
faq.answer: GCSE coverage is built in. Other qualifications can be added through custom setup.

card.title: GCSE revision planner
card.description: See how Study Hour plans revision around exam timing, weak topics, and recent study.
